data.text: The unit suite passed. The E2E build failed under parallel load with an out-of-memory dependency load in the app server, so I’m re-running the browser suite in isolation to see whether it’s a resource issue or a real regression.
The app server is up and the E2E harness is live now. I’m waiting for the actual browser assertions to finish, since the previous failure was only the build phase under parallel load.
The suite is progressing normally now. I’m watching for the new `/agent` cases to surface at the end so I can confirm they pass and not just assume the redirect fix is covered.

The run is healthy. The existing sessions compatibility test is still passing, which is a good sign that the helper change didn’t disturb the old route; I’m waiting for the new `/agent` cases to come through.

Both new assertions failed, so I’m pulling the exact mismatch now. The helper change is likely fine; this is probably about how Playwright reports the redirect chain or how the fallback route is being resolved in the browser context.

I’ve confirmed the rest of the suite is stable. The failures are isolated to the two new navigation assertions, so I’m waiting for Playwright to print the exact assertion diff before I tighten the checks.

The route behavior itself is still suspect, but I need the concrete assertion failure before I change the spec. I’m reading the Playwright result artifacts directly so I can see whether this is a redirect-chain mismatch, a response object mismatch, or a fallback-route mismatch.

data.text: The failure is clear now: the current test is inspecting the wrong thing. `/agent` is loading as a page and redirecting on the client side in this harness, so I need to assert the navigation sequence instead of expecting `page.goto()` to hand back the final redirected request. I’m rewriting the two tests around browser navigation events and the final URL.

I’ve changed the spec to observe the browser navigation sequence instead of the raw response object. The long suite still has the old failure run in flight, so I’m letting it drain and then I’ll run just the `/agent` cases against the updated test.

The full E2E suite has unrelated screenshot and scale-fixture failures in this environment, so I’m narrowing to the two navigation cases I changed. That lets me validate the bead itself without conflating it with preexisting suite noise.

data.text: The focused run is still failing, but now it’s only these two tests. I’m grabbing the assertion details so I can see whether the browser ever reaches the final URL or whether the route is staying put on `/agent`.

I’ve got the shape of the fix now: keep the browser test focused on the live `/agent` URL transition and cover the fallback path with a helper unit test, because the no-project browser mock is not intercepting the server-side fetch in this harness. I’m adding that helper test and simplifying the Playwright assertion so it checks the real user-visible behavior.

I’m splitting the verification along the seam the code actually has: the live browser test will validate `/agent` lands on the final run URL without a `/sessions` network hop, and a unit test will cover the empty-project fallback on `resolveDefaultProjectRoute`. That keeps the browser test honest without depending on a server-side mock it can’t intercept.
The changes are in. I’m verifying the helper unit test first, then I’ll rerun only the `/agent` Playwright case so we can see the exact browser-level behavior for the direct hop.

The route change is implemented, but I did not commit it because the bead’s full e2e gate is still red for unrelated reasons.

- `/agent` now redirects directly to `runs?layer=run` in [`cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/agent/+page.ts`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-9970925c-20260607T133847-dd47dfe9/cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/agent/+page.ts).

- I added coverage in [`cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/routing/shellRoutes.test.ts`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-9970925c-20260607T133847-dd47dfe9/cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/routing/shellRoutes.test.ts).
